cxu_object.claim: High-frequency trading on Hyperliquid will rapidly destroy profits through fee accumulation because every trade incurs a percentage-based fee on notional value and the round-trip cost of entering and exiting a position must be estimated and subtracted from expected profit before any trade is placed

supporting_contexts.text: Hyperliquid perpetual fees are charged as a percentage of notional trade value. At Tier 0 (base rate): taker fee is 0.045% (4.5 bps) and maker rebate is 0.015% (1.5 bps). Volume-based tiers reduce fees: Tier 1 (>$5M 14d volume) taker 0.040%, Tier 2 (>$25M) taker 0.035%, down to Tier 6 (>$7B) taker 0.024%. Maker rebates phase out at Tier 4+. HYPE staking provides additional 5-40% discount depending on amount staked.

Round-trip fee calculation: for a taker entry and taker exit at Tier 0, total cost is 0.045% + 0.045% = 0.09% of notional. On a $10,000 position this is $9. On a $15,000 position this is $13.50. If using maker orders for one leg, cost reduces to 0.045% - 0.015% = 0.03% per leg. The price must move at least the round-trip fee percentage before the trade becomes profitable. Source: https://hyperliquid.gitbook.io/hyperliquid-docs/trading/fees

Observed during one week of live trading: $64 in fees paid on only $5 gross profit resulting in -$59 net. Strategies with high trade frequency (Edge Detection at 15-25 trades/day, Scalp Bands at 20-30 trades/day) consistently lost money to fee drag while Signal Consensus at 2-3 trades/day was the only consistently profitable approach.

takerFeePct.description: Taker fee as percentage of notional (Tier 0 = 0.045%, Tier 6 = 0.024%)

makerRebatePct.description: Maker rebate as percentage of notional (Tier 0 = 0.015%, phases out at Tier 4+)

currentTier.description: Current fee tier based on rolling 14-day weighted volume

version.change_description: Renamed from axiom-fee-kill. Rewrote claim to focus on fee accumulation risk with high-frequency trading. Added official Hyperliquid fee schedule with tier structure, maker/taker rates, and round-trip calculation formula. Added parameters for current fee tier.
